messages.content: Create me a simple Unity game in 2D with a top view. The circle character should WASD in all directions, and food circles will spawn around him. The most important condition is to add ML Agents and then explain to me how to set everything up. If anything, I have Unity and ML Agents installed and imported. I only need the code and instructions on how to make the training of the neural network start, as well as how to switch to keyboard control. The code must be complete and working.

When i modified PlayerController, Behavior Parameters appeared in the inspector. What should I write in it? Also what should I do with the Behavior Parameters in the Training Area now? Why did I specify the parameters in the Training Area at all, if the PlayerController will be trained, and not the Training Area.

Apologies for the confusion. You should add "Behavior Parameters" and "Decision Requester" components in the "Player" GameObject instead of the "Training Area" GameObject. Then you can set the behavior parameters in the Player GameObject. Here's the updated process:

1. Remove "Behavior Parameters" and "Decision Requester" components from the "Training Area" GameObject.

2. Add "Behavior Parameters" and "Decision Requester" components to the "Player" GameObject. Configure as following:

      - Behavior Name: "PlayerLearning"
      - Vector Observation > Space Size: 2
      - Actions > Space Type: Continuous
      - Actions > Space Size: 2
      - Model: Drag "PlayerBrain" here

   And for "Decision Requester":

      - Decision Period: 5
      - Take Actions Between Decisions: True

The "Training Area" GameObject represents the environment in which your agent will learn. In this case, it includes the Food and Player objects, as well as the boundary for spawning food. Although PlayerController is being trained, the training process occurs within the Training Area.

To clarify: You should set the behavior parameters in the Player GameObject, as the agent's learning is controlled through the PlayerController script attached to the Player itself. The Training Area GameObject is only there to give a logical area for the food spawning and other environment-specific functionalities.
When I press Play, I just have a huge food prefab right in the middle of the Training Area. I had a fixed size, and apparently FoodSpawner does not properly spawn it.
player can't eat this food. Also in observation there are no food coordinates or anything else. There is also no reward for eating food.

1) When i start eating food, i get yellow errors in console:
Fewer observations (12) made than vector observation size (22). The observations will be padded.

2) the player moves too smoothly, as if he is on ice. make his movements sharper.
What about episodes? How to set the maximum episode time? How to end an episode when the food runs out? I would also like to create 20 areas at once in order to train 20 times faster.

when i'm not in Play mode, i get yellow in console:
Assets\Scripts\PlayerController.cs(73,17): warning CS0108: 'PlayerController.EndEpisode()' hides inherited member 'Agent.EndEpisode()'. Use the new keyword if hiding was intended.

when i eat all food i get a lot of red errors in console:
StackOverflowException: The requested operation caused a stack overflow.
PlayerController.EndEpisode () (at Assets/Scripts/PlayerController.cs:75)
what about if the episode had a time limit in seconds? where can i enter it? in yaml? what if I have multiple Training Areas? Will everything work properly?
ok, now how to create invisible walls so the player can't go outside? whether it is necessary to register it in observation? I have already created a black 2D Square and named it "Floor".

To create invisible walls, follow these steps:

1. In the Hierarchy, create an empty GameObject and name it "Walls".
2. Create a new 2D square sprite by right-clicking in the Assets panel > Create > Sprites > Square. This square will be used to create invisible walls.
3. In the Hierarchy, create a new GameObject as a child of the "Walls" GameObject, name it "TopWall", and add a Sprite Renderer component with the created square sprite as Sprite. 
4. Add a "Box Collider 2D" component to the "TopWall" GameObject; make sure the "Is Trigger" checkbox is unchecked.
5. Scale the top wall to fit the width of your Floor GameObject and position it at the top edge of the Floor. You can modify the size of the collider in the Box Collider 2D component to customize the wall's thickness.
6. Similarly, create "BottomWall", "LeftWall", and "RightWall" GameObjects. Position and scale them to fit the respective edges of the Floor GameObject.
7. If you want the walls to be invisible, disable the Sprite Renderer component on each wall GameObject.

You don't need to add the walls in observation since they are static elements that won't change during the game. The agent should quickly learn not to collide with the walls just by interacting with them.

Now with the invisible walls set up, the player won't be able to go outside the Floor area.
when I start copying the Training Area so that I have many areas during training, all food from all areas is created in the very first one.

your yaml file not works:
behaviors:
  PlayerLearning:
    trainer_type: ppo
    max_steps: 100000
    time_horizon: 64
    learning_rate: 0.0003
    batch_size: 128
    buffer_size: 4096
    discount: 0.99
    beta: 0.01
    strength: 0.5
    steps_until_spawn: 0
    summary_freq: 1000

it gives an error in terminal:
mlagents.trainers.exception.TrainerConfigError: The option learning_rate was specified in your YAML file for TrainerSettings, but is invalid.

My apologies for the inconvenience. It seems that the error is due to a change in ML-Agents package version, where trainer settings have been reorganized. Here's the correct YAML configuration for ML-Agents v1.0:

```yaml
behaviors:
  PlayerLearning:
    trainer_type: ppo
    max_steps: 100000
    time_horizon: 64
    summary_freq: 1000
    hyperparameters:
      learning_rate: 3.0e-4
      batch_size: 128
      buffer_size: 4096
      discount: 0.99
      beta: 0.01
      strength: 0.5
      num_epoch: 3
    network_settings:
      hidden_units: 128
      num_layers: 2
```

Replace the previous YAML configuration with the new one above. After updating your YAML file, try training your agent again, and it should work without any errors.

Please note that it's always a good practice to double-check the specific ML-Agents version you are using as configurations and required syntax might vary between different versions. Find ML-Agents' "installation.md" documentation to understand the version-specific configuration file syntax.

new error:
mlagents.trainers.exception.TrainerConfigError: The option discount was specified in your YAML file for PPOSettings, but is invalid.

ok, i'll give you modern yaml file from latest version. here's for example Basic.yaml:
behaviors:
  Basic:
    trainer_type: ppo
    hyperparameters:
      batch_size: 32
      buffer_size: 256
      learning_rate: 0.0003
      beta: 0.005
      epsilon: 0.2
      lambd: 0.95
      num_epoch: 3
      learning_rate_schedule: linear
    network_settings:
      normalize: false
      hidden_units: 20
      num_layers: 1
      vis_encode_type: simple
    reward_signals:
      extrinsic:
        gamma: 0.9
        strength: 1.0
    keep_checkpoints: 5
    max_steps: 500000
    time_horizon: 3
    summary_freq: 2000

Thank you for sharing the YAML file. Here's the adjusted version of the YAML file for the PlayerLearning behavior:

```yaml
behaviors:
  PlayerLearning:
    trainer_type: ppo
    hyperparameters:
      batch_size: 32
      buffer_size: 256
      learning_rate: 0.0003
      beta: 0.005
      epsilon: 0.2
      lambd: 0.95
      num_epoch: 3
      learning_rate_schedule: linear
    network_settings:
      normalize: false
      hidden_units: 20
      num_layers: 2
    reward_signals:
      extrinsic:
        gamma: 0.99
        strength: 1.0
    keep_checkpoints: 5
    max_steps: 100000
    time_horizon: 64
    summary_freq: 1000
```

Let me explain the changes I made to adapt it to your case:

- The `Basic` behavior key has been replaced with `PlayerLearning`.
- The `time_horizon` value has been changed back to 64, for learning a more extended time horizon in your environment.
- The `max_steps` value has been changed back to 100000 as suggested in the original YAML file.
- `num_layers` in the `network_settings` section has been changed back to 2 as suggested in the original YAML file.

Please use this YAML file, and your training should run without errors. Note that the specific hyperparameters might need tuning to achieve good learning performance. The necessary changes depend on your custom agent and environment.
